slides.title: Exploring Numbers up to 10,000
slides.text:
As numbers grow, we add more columns to the left in our place value chart.
For 4-digit numbers, we have: Thousands (Th), Hundreds (H), Tens (T), and Ones (O).
For instance, consider the example of Example:
In 4,528: 4 is in the Thousands place.
Another application is demonstrated in science classrooms.
Verify these concepts by testing them with simple setups.
How does the concept of Exploring Numbers up to 10,000 help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: Example
examples.content: In 4,528: 4 is in the Thousands place.

slides.title: Comparing Numbers
slides.text:
To compare numbers, we look at the digits in the highest place value column first.
We use comparison symbols: '>' (greater than), '<' (less than), and '=' (equal to).
For instance, consider the example of Example:
Comparing 3,456 and 3,429: 3,456 > 3,429 since 5 tens > 2 tens.
Another application is demonstrated in science classrooms.
Verify these concepts by testing them with simple setups.
How does the concept of Comparing Numbers help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: Example
examples.content: Comparing 3,456 and 3,429: 3,456 > 3,429 since 5 tens > 2 tens.

slides.title: Ordering Numbers
slides.text:
Ordering numbers can be done in Ascending Order (from smallest to largest) or Descending Order (from largest to smallest).
This slide introduces key insights about Ordering Numbers.
For instance, consider the example of Example:
Ascending: 125, 456, 789. Descending: 789, 456, 125.
Another application is demonstrated in science classrooms.
Verify these concepts by testing them with simple setups.
How does the concept of Ordering Numbers help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: Example
examples.content: Ascending: 125, 456, 789. Descending: 789, 456, 125.

slides.title: Place Value in 4-Digit Numbers
slides.text:
Understanding place value up to 10,000 helps us with addition and subtraction of large numbers.
For example, 1,000 has 10 hundreds.
For instance, consider the example of Example:
In 8,025: 8 is worth 8,000.
Another application is demonstrated in science classrooms.
Verify these concepts by testing them with simple setups.
How does the concept of Place Value in 4-Digit Numbers help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: Example
examples.content: In 8,025: 8 is worth 8,000.

slides.title: Summary of Comparing
slides.text:
Remember: Always compare starting from the leftmost column.
If they are equal, move to the next column on the right.
For instance, consider the example of Example:
9,812 < 9,820 because 1 ten < 2 tens.
Another application is demonstrated in science classrooms.
Verify these concepts by testing them with simple setups.
How does the concept of Summary of Comparing help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: Example
examples.content: 9,812 < 9,820 because 1 ten < 2 tens.

2. 9,105 _____ 8,999

Exercise 2: Ordering Sequences

Arrange the numbers in the order specified.

1. Arrange in Ascending Order: 3,450; 3,045; 3,540; 3,405
3,045; 3,405; 3,450; 3,540
2. Arrange in Descending Order: 8,129; 8,912; 8,219; 8,192

Exercise 3: Greatest and Least Values

Use the digits 3, 8, 0, 5 once each to write the requested numbers.

1. What is the largest 4-digit number you can make?

2. What is the smallest 4-digit number you can make? (Remember it must start with a non-zero digit).
3,058

science.title: Science
science.subtitle: Week 2: Asking Questions Like a Scientist

slides.title: Welcome to Inquiry!
slides.text:
Hello, Explorer! Welcome to Science Week 2!
This week we learn to ask questions like real scientists.
Scientists are curious about everything they see in nature.
They start their investigations by asking why and how.
We will learn how to tinker with common materials.
Tinkering means touching and testing things to see changes.
Let's prepare our curiosity and get ready to ask.
Are you ready to explore and learn with us today?

examples.title: Goal
examples.content: Formulate scientific questions by exploring and tinkering with materials.

slides.title: Curiosity and Inquiry
slides.text:
Inquiry is the heart of all scientific discoveries.
It begins when you observe something and ask questions.
Why does the ice cube melt on the wooden desk?
How does a heavy metal key sink in the water?
Curiosity drives us to search for answers in nature.
We ask questions that we can test through experiments.
Avoid asking opinions, like 'Is this flower beautiful?'
Let's practice forming questions about what we see today!

examples.title: Inquiry
examples.content: Asking: 'Why does wet sand feel colder than dry sand?'

slides.title: Tinkering with Materials
slides.text:
Tinkering is a hands-on way to explore physical things.
We play with materials to observe how they behave.
We press clay, stretch bands, and drop heavy keys.
This action helps us see what properties materials hold.
Squeezing a dry sponge shows it has air holes.
Squeezing a wet sponge shows it holds liquid inside.
Tinkering helps us form solid questions for tests.
Tinker with three objects in your room safely today!

examples.title: Tinkering
examples.content: Pressing modeling clay with a spoon to leave hollow shapes.

slides.title: Tinkering with Modeling Clay
slides.text:
Modeling clay is a solid material we can shape.
Tinkering with clay shows it changes shape easily.
We press it, roll it, and pinch it with fingers.
The clay holds the new shape we give to it.
Why does clay stay bent while rubber bands snap back?
This is a great scientific question to investigate.
We apply force using our hands to shape the clay.
Observe how your stylus can press clay to write names!

examples.title: Clay Test
examples.content: Pressing a fork into clay leaves a lined print on its surface.

slides.title: Tinkering with Balloons
slides.text:
Balloons are elastic materials that hold invisible air.
Tinkering with balloons shows air occupies physical space.
Blowing air inside makes the balloon stretch and expand.
The air pushes outwards against the balloon walls.
This pushing force is called air pressure in science.
Squeezing the inflated balloon changes its outer shape.
Letting go of the neck makes the balloon fly away.
Why does releasing the air make the balloon move?

examples.title: Balloon Test
examples.content: Air escaping from the neck pushes the balloon forward.

slides.title: Tinkering with Sponges
slides.text:
A dry sponge is light and full of tiny holes.
These holes are filled with invisible air molecules.
Dipping the sponge in water shows absorption properties.
The sponge sucks in liquid, displacing the air inside.
You will observe tiny air bubbles rising in water.
Squeezing the wet sponge releases the absorbed water.
A wet sponge feels much heavier than a dry one.
Why does water occupy the empty holes of sponges?

examples.title: Sponge Test
examples.content: Squeezing a wet sponge returns it to its light, dry state.

slides.title: Tinkering with Magnets
slides.text:
Magnets hold an invisible force called magnetism.
Tinkering with magnets shows they pull certain metals.
They pull iron nails, paperclips, and steel keys.
They do not pull wooden sticks or plastic toys.
This pulling force is called magnetic attraction in science.
Magnets can pull objects even through thin paper.
Why do magnets only attract iron and steel metals?
Test different objects in your room with magnets safely!

examples.title: Magnet Test
examples.content: A magnet pulls steel paperclips across the desk surface.

slides.title: Tinkering with Paper Planes
slides.text:
Folding paper into planes shows aerodynamics in action.
Tinkering with shapes changes how far planes fly.
Thin, pointed planes cut through air very fast.
Wide, flat planes float slowly but land soon.
Air pushes against the paper wings to lift planes.
This upward pushing force is called air resistance.
Why does a pointed nose make planes fly further?
Fold two different plane shapes and test them now!

examples.title: Plane Test
examples.content: Comparing a pointed dart plane with a wide glider plane.

slides.title: Tinkering with Floating Objects
slides.text:
Tinkering with water shows objects float or sink.
Buoyancy determines if things stay on top or go down.
Dry leaves, twigs, and plastic spoons float on water.
Iron keys, stones, and glass marbles sink to bottoms.
Why do heavy metal keys sink while large logs float?
This question leads to density concepts in science.
Test a coin and a plastic bottle cap in buckets.
Observe which one floats and which one sinks down!

examples.title: Float Test
examples.content: A plastic bottle cap floats because it is light and holds air.

slides.title: Tinkering with Dissolving Solids
slides.text:
Mixing solids in water shows how they dissolve.
Tinkering with sugar reveals it mixes completely.
Sugar crystals become invisible when stirred in water.
This mixtures is called a solution in daily science.
Does sugar dissolve faster in hot or cold water?
Stirring makes sugar dissolve much quicker than sitting.
Why does heat speed up the dissolving process?
Observe how salt behaves when stirred in water jars!

examples.title: Dissolving Test
examples.content: Stirring sugar in warm water makes it dissolve in seconds.

slides.title: Tinkering with Flashlights
slides.text:
Tinkering with light sources changes shadow sizes.
Shine a flashlight at a toy figurine on tables.
Observe the dark shadow formed on the wall behind.
Moving the light closer makes the shadow grow larger.
Moving the light further away makes the shadow smaller.
Tilting the flashlight changes the shadow's direction.
Why does moving the light change the shadow's scale?
Draw the different shadow sizes on your paper page!

examples.title: Light Test
examples.content: Closest light source creates the largest and dimmest shadow.

slides.title: Tinkering with Spinning Tops
slides.text:
Spinning tops show rotational motion and friction.
We spin a plastic top on a smooth table.
Circular force keeps the top standing upright while spinning.
The tip of the top rubs against table surfaces.
This rubbing force is called friction in daily science.
Friction slows down the top until it falls over.
Why does a smooth table make tops spin longer?
Compare spinning on rough carpets and smooth tables!

examples.title: Spin Test
examples.content: Rough surfaces create high friction, stopping tops quickly.

slides.title: Tinkering with Mirrors
slides.text:
Mirrors reflect light to show clear images.
A mirror has a flat, extremely smooth surface.
Light bounces off this surface back to your eyes.
Tilting the mirror changes what objects you observe.
Why do we see our reflection in glass mirrors?
Rough wood does not reflect light to show images.
Rough surfaces scatter light waves in all directions.
Explore reflections using shiny metal spoons in kitchens!

examples.title: Mirror Test
examples.content: Bouncing light off smooth surfaces creates reflection images.

slides.title: Tinkering with Elastic Bands
slides.text:
Rubber bands stretch when we apply pulling forces.
This property of materials is called elasticity.
The band snaps back to its shape when released.
Plucking a stretched band creates rapid vibrations.
These vibrations produce a hum sound in the air.
Stretching the band tighter makes a higher pitch sound.
Why does tension change the pitch of sound waves?
Hold one end with teeth and pluck bands safely!

examples.title: Band Test
examples.content: Vibrating rubber bands create sound waves we hear easily.

slides.title: Tinkering with Bent Stems
slides.text:
Plants respond to light sources in their environment.
Tinker with a small potted plant near windows.
Observe that stems bend towards the bright sunlight.
Turn the pot around so stems face rooms.
In a few days, stems bend back to windows.
Why do plant stems grow towards the light source?
Plants need sunlight to survive and make food.
This growth response is called phototropism in science!

examples.title: Stem Test
examples.content: Stems bend towards sunlight to maximize light absorption.

slides.title: Tinkering with Melting Ice
slides.text:
Ice cubes melt into liquid water at room temperature.
Tinker with ice cubes placed in different spots.
Put one cube in direct sun and one in shade.
The cube in the sun melts in minutes completely.
The cube in the shade melts much slower today.
Why does solar heat melt ice so rapidly?
Heat energy breaks the solid bonds of ice crystals.
Measure the melting times using clocks on desks!

examples.title: Ice Test
examples.content: Direct heat increases melting speed by breaking solid bonds.

slides.title: Tinkering with Soil Textures
slides.text:
We group rocks and soils by physical properties.
Tinker with a pile of dry garden rocks.
Feel their textures with your finger tips closely.
Sort them into rough stones and smooth river pebbles.
Why are river pebbles so smooth and rounded?
Water flows over them, wearing down rough edges.
Classifying rocks by texture is a science skill.
Write down the rock groups in your log book!

examples.title: Rock Test
examples.content: River stones are smoothed by friction from flowing water.

slides.title: Tinkering with Sprouting Seeds
slides.text:
Seeds need specific conditions to start growing.
Tinker with seeds placed in two soil pots.
Keep one pot damp and one completely dry.
Seeds in damp soil sprout green roots soon.
Seeds in dry soil stay asleep and do not grow.
Why do seeds need water to sprout roots?
Water activates the seed embryo to start life.
Record sprout heights in both pots daily!

examples.title: Seed Test
examples.content: Moisture triggers germination, ending the seed's rest state.

slides.title: Guided Practice: Forming Why Questions
slides.text:
Let's practice forming scientific 'Why' questions.
Look at a puddle of water drying up soon.
Observe that heat from the Sun warms the water.
Form the question: 'Why does heat cause evaporation?'
This question asks about the cause of changes.
It can be investigated by testing warm and cold water.
Avoid questions like: 'Is puddle water happy?'
Always search for causes when you ask why!

examples.title: Why Question
examples.content: Why does heat speed up the melting of solid butter?

slides.title: Guided Practice: Forming How Questions
slides.text:
Let's practice forming scientific 'How' questions.
Look at how green plants grow from dry seeds.
Observe that plants grow roots and stems soon.
Form: 'How does water help seeds break shells?'
This question asks about the steps of processes.
It can be answered by observing seeds under lenses.
Avoid questions like: 'Do seeds dream in soil?'
Always ask about processes when you ask how!

examples.title: How Question
examples.content: How does a magnet pull steel paperclips through paper?

slides.title: Guided Practice: Squeezing Sponges
slides.text:
Let's explore sponges in water like real scientists.
Observe what happens when you squeeze a sponge.
Squeeze it under water in a clear glass bowl.
You will observe bubbles rising to the top.
Why do bubbles rise when sponges are squeezed?
The water enters the sponge, pushing air out.
Air is lighter than water, so bubbles float up.
Record this bubble observation in your journal page!

examples.title: Observation
examples.content: Squeezing releases trapped air, which floats up as bubbles.

slides.title: Guided Practice: Water on Leaves
slides.text:
Let's explore how water behaves on different leaves.
Drip water drops on a shiny gabi leaf.
Observe that water rolls off like small beads.
Now drip water on a dry paper leaf on desk.
Observe that water soaks in, wetting the paper.
Why does water roll off gabi leaves completely?
The waxy coating on the leaf blocks water absorption.
This waxy property is waterproof, protecting the plant!

examples.title: Observation
examples.content: Waxy surfaces repel water, preventing absorption in leaves.

slides.title: Common Error: Vague Questions
slides.text:
Avoid asking questions that cannot be tested.
Scientists do not ask: 'Do balloons feel pain?'
This question is vague and cannot be measured.
Instead, ask: 'How does air amount affect balloon size?'
This question can be measured using rulers easily.
Always ask questions about physical properties and facts.
Make sure your questions have measurable answers.
Check your questions before you start experiments!

examples.title: Incorrect
examples.content: Asking: 'Is clay happy when we mold it into shapes?'

slides.title: Common Error: Guessing Without Tests
slides.text:
Do not just guess outcomes without performing tests.
Tinkering and exploring are necessary to gather facts.
Guessing that a key floats is not scientific.
Place the key in water to verify if it sinks.
Observations provide evidence to answer questions.
Always test materials before drawing conclusions.
Evidence makes scientific explanations reliable and solid.
Never write down guesses as factual observations!

examples.title: Incorrect
examples.content: Writing 'Stones float' in your log without testing them.

slides.title: Summary of Week 2
slides.text:
Congratulations! You completed all the slides for Week 2.
Let's review the main science tools we mastered.
We learned that curiosity drives scientific inquiry.
We ask 'Why' and 'How' questions to start tests.
We tinker with clay, sponges, and magnets to explore.
We observe properties like floating, sinking, and melting.
We avoid vague questions and test before guessing.
You are now ready to take the quiz and challenges!

examples.title: Key Takeaway
examples.content: Tinkering with materials helps us form testable questions.

standard.q: What do scientists do when they are curious about a material?
options: They ask questions and tinker with it.

standard.q: Which of the following is a good 'Why' question for an ice cube?
options: Why does ice melt when it gets warm?

standard.q: What happens to clay when you squeeze it in your hand?
Why does a balloon get bigger when you blow air into it?
options: Air takes up space and pushes outwards.

standard.q: Which of the following materials is waterproof (does not absorb water)?
What kind of question starts an exploration of magnets?
options: What materials can a magnet pull?

standard.q: What property are we testing when we drop a stone and a leaf in water?
How does sugar behave when mixed into warm water?
options: It dissolves and disappears from view.

standard.q: Which question helps us investigate shadows using a flashlight?
options: How does the distance of the light change the shadow's size?

standard.q: What happens to a spinning top when it rubs against a rough table?
What bounces off a mirror to show your reflection?
How can you make a stretched rubber band produce a higher sound?
In what direction do plant stems grow when placed near a sunny window?
options: Towards the light of the window
They do not grow at all.

standard.q: Where does an ice cube melt the fastest?
options: In a cup of cold water

standard.q: True or False: Asking 'How does water help seeds sprout?' is a scientific question.
What do we do when we 'tinker' with materials?
options: We read a storybook about them.
We touch, play, and experiment with them to see what happens.
We buy them from the market.
We throw them in the trash bin.

standard.q: Which of these objects will sink to the bottom of a water bucket?
Why does a sponge feel heavier after you dip it in water?
options: The sponge absorbs water into its holes.

standard.q: True or False: A scientist only asks questions that can be tested by observing or experimenting.
Which question is NOT scientific because it is about personal taste?
options: Does a paper plane fly further when folded thin?
Is science the most beautiful subject?
Does sugar dissolve faster in hot water?
Which materials are attracted to magnets?

standard.q: What happens to a dry sponge when you squeeze it under water?
options: Air bubbles escape and water goes inside.

standard.q: Why does water roll off a gabi leaf in beads?
options: The leaf is waxy and waterproof.

standard.q: Which VCV word represents a testable guess we form after asking questions?
True or False: Squeezing modeling clay changes its shape, but not its weight.
What do we call the result of our tinkering and testing?

challenge.q: If you observe a balloon getting smaller over three days, what is the best scientific question to ask?
options: Why does air slowly escape from the rubber balloon?
Does the balloon prefer being small?
Who drew a face on the balloon?

challenge.q: You tinker with a metal key and a plastic key in the sun. The metal key feels much hotter. Why?
options: Metal conducts and absorbs heat better than plastic.
The sun only shines on metal.

challenge.q: A student asks: 'Why do plants bend towards light?' How can we test this?
options: Put plants in a box with a single hole for light.
Sing songs to the plants in the dark.
Water the plants with colored juice.

challenge.q: Which of these is a testable question about magnets?
options: How many sheets of paper can a magnetic force pull through?
Why are magnets so cool to play with?

challenge.q: What can you infer when a dry sponge placed in oil does not release bubbles?
options: The sponge was already wet or oil behaves differently.

performance.title: Everyday Material Tinkering Log
performance.desc: Tinker with two materials at home (e.g., a plastic bottle cap and a steel spoon). Put them in water, place them in the sun, and touch them. Write two 'Why' or 'How' questions based on your observations, and explain what properties (floating, heat conduction) you tested to your parent.
labels: Conducted hands-on tinkering with two materials
Formulated two testable scientific questions (Why/How)
Correctly explained material properties based on tests

pages:
Exercise 1: Forming Inquiry Questions

Write a testable scientific question for each observation using Why or How.

1. Observation: An ice cube melts faster on a metal plate than a wood board.
Why does metal melt ice faster than wood?
2. Observation: Magnets pull steel paperclips but not copper pennies.
How do magnets attract paperclips but not pennies?

Exercise 2: Tinkering Observations

Write the correct observation result (Floats, Sinks, or Melts) for each activity.

1. Placing an iron nail in a cup of river water:
Sinks
2. Placing a dry bamboo twig on a quiet pond:
Floats
3. Placing solid butter on a warm pan:
Melts

Exercise 3: Clay and Elasticity Puzzles

Answer the questions using physical facts about clay and rubber bands.

Describe what happens to a rubber band when you stretch and release it:
It stretches when pulled and snaps back to its original shape.
This is because the rubber band has elastic properties.

slides.title: Welcome to the Animal Kingdom!
slides.text:
Hello, Explorer! Welcome to English Week 2!
This week, we are diving into the world of action.
We will learn about words that start with double consonants.
We will understand action verbs and how they show movement.
We will ask questions like a scientist using question marks.
We will also learn to read character feelings and traits.
Let's keep our eyes open and watch animals in action.
Are you ready to explore the animal kingdom today?

examples.title: Weekly Goal
examples.content: Master CCVC words, understand action verbs, ask question sentences, and infer feelings.

slides.title: Starting Sounds (CCVC)
slides.text:
CCVC words stand for Consonant-Consonant-Vowel-Consonant.
They start with a blend of two consonant sounds.
Think of the word f-r-o-g. The 'f' and 'r' blend.
We say them together quickly: frog!
CCVC words are different from CVCC words.
They have their consonant blends at the beginning.
Let's practice saying beginning consonant blends today.
They help us name many interesting things in nature!

examples.title: Word Pattern
examples.content: C = Consonant, C = Consonant, V = Vowel, C = Consonant.

slides.title: Phonics Hunt: The Green Frog
slides.text:
In the wet pond, we observe a green frog.
The word 'frog' starts with the 'fr' blend.
We watch how it leaps from leaf to leaf.
We spell the word as f - r - o - g.
Listen to the beginning sound of the word 'frog'.
The letters 'f' and 'r' join to make 'fr'.
It is a wonderful CCVC word we see in ponds.
Let's say 'frog' aloud and feel the blend!

examples.title: Spelling
examples.content: f - r - o - g

slides.title: Phonics Hunt: The Crawling Crab
slides.text:
On the sandy beach, we spot a small crab.
It crawls sideways along the wet sand.
The word 'crab' starts with the 'cr' blend.
We spell the word as c - r - a - b.
How many legs does a crab use to crawl?
Crabs are fascinating creatures in the shoreline habitat.
Say 'crab' to hear the 'cr' starting sound.
It is another CCVC word in our nature list!

examples.title: Spelling
examples.content: c - r - a - b

slides.title: Phonics Hunt: In the Pond
slides.text:
We watch a small fish swim in the water.
The word 'swim' starts with the blend 'sw'.
We must not block the drain plug in the sink.
The word 'plug' starts with the blend 'pl'.
Both 'swim' and 'plug' are CCVC spelling patterns.
They help us describe actions and useful objects.
We spell them as s-w-i-m and p-l-u-g.
Practice writing these words in your notebook now!

examples.title: Spelling
examples.content: s - w - i - m  and  p - l - u - g

slides.title: Phonics Hunt: Music and Play
slides.text:
Hear the loud beat of the parade drum!
The word 'drum' starts with the blend 'dr'.
Red and blue flags wave in the blowing wind.
The word 'flag' starts with the blend 'fl'.
Both 'drum' and 'flag' add color to our day.
They follow the CCVC word spelling structure.
We spell them as d-r-u-m and f-l-a-g.
Can you hear the starting sounds of drum and flag?

examples.title: Spelling
examples.content: d - r - u - m  and  f - l - a - g

slides.title: Phonics Hunt: Safety First
slides.text:
We see a red stop sign on the paved road.
The word 'stop' starts with the blend 'st'.
We must not slip on the wet bathroom floor.
The word 'slip' starts with the blend 'sl'.
Both 'stop' and 'slip' help keep us safe.
They are common CCVC words used in instructions.
We spell them as s-t-o-p and s-l-i-p.
Be careful to say the starting consonants clearly!

examples.title: Spelling
examples.content: s - t - o - p  and  s - l - i - p

slides.title: Phonics Hunt: Flat Leaves
slides.text:
The leaves of the water lily are green and flat.
The word 'flat' starts with the blend 'fl'.
Be careful not to fall into a deep mud trap!
The word 'trap' starts with the blend 'tr'.
Both 'flat' and 'trap' describe physical things.
They follow the CCVC word pattern in phonics.
We spell them as f-l-a-t and t-r-a-p.
Let's practice writing them on our paper today!

examples.title: Spelling
examples.content: f - l - a - t  and  t - r - a - p

slides.title: Grammar: Action Words (Verbs)
slides.text:
Verbs are words that show action or movement.
They tell us what people, animals, or things do.
Without verbs, nothing in our stories would move!
Verbs are the motor engines of our sentences.
They show actions like run, jump, sing, and play.
In science, we use verbs to describe natural events.
E.g., 'The wind blows' or 'The water boils'.
Let's find verbs in the sentences we read!

examples.title: Key Concept
examples.content: Verb = Action word

slides.title: Observing Animals in Action
slides.text:
Let's watch animals in their natural habitats.
The green frog leaps across the pond.
The blue bird flies high in the clear sky.
The gold fish swims in the cool stream.
The action verbs are leaps, flies, and swims.
They describe the movements of living things.
Using specific verbs makes our observations clear.
What actions can you observe a dog performing?

examples.title: Action
examples.content: leap, fly, swim, crawl, run

slides.title: Verbs in Our Daily Life
slides.text:
We use verbs to talk about what we do.
'Matteo reads a story book in the room.'
'The children play at school during recess.'
'My sister writes a science report on paper.'
The verbs are reads, play, and writes.
They tell us the actions of our characters.
Notice how the verb changes for one or many.
Matteo reads (one), but the children play (many).

examples.title: Matteo
examples.content: reads

examples.title: Children
examples.content: play

slides.title: Sentence Engine: Interrogative Sentences
slides.text:
An interrogative sentence is an asking sentence.
It asks a question to gather facts or details.
It must always start with a capital letter.
It must always end with a question mark (?).
Scientists use interrogative sentences to investigate nature.
They ask questions about how the world works.
Let's learn how to ask questions in English.
It is the first step to becoming a scientist!

examples.title: Rule
examples.content: Always start with a capital letter and end with a question mark.

slides.title: Question Words
slides.text:
We start questions with special interrogative words.
These words are: Who, What, Where, When, and Why.
'Who is looking at the green frog in the pond?'
'What does the crawling crab eat on the beach?'
'Where does the water flow when it rains?'
Each word asks for a different kind of detail.
'Where' asks for place, 'What' asks for things.
Which question word asks about the reason why?

examples.title: Question Words
examples.content: Who, What, Where, When, Why

slides.title: Story Time: The Pond Detective
slides.text:
Let's read a short story about Matteo's discovery:
'Matteo looks closely at the quiet pond.
He asks, 'Who is hiding under the green leaf?'
Suddenly, a green frog leaps out of the water!
Matteo laughs and says, 'My frog friend is safe!''
This story has both telling and asking sentences.
The question helps us find out what happens next.
Let's look at the character's feelings next!

examples.title: Reading Task
examples.content: Identify the CCVC words and verbs in this story!

slides.title: Inferring Feelings: Happy or Scared?
slides.text:
To infer means to find clues in the story.
We use these clues to understand what is not said.
In our story, Matteo laughs when the frog leaps.
He says, 'My frog friend is safe!' in a happy voice.
We can infer that Matteo feels happy and relieved.
He is not scared of the leaping green frog at all.
Paying attention to actions helps us infer feelings.
How do you feel when you see a frog leap?

examples.title: Infer
examples.content: Matteo laughs -> Matteo is happy.

slides.title: Inferring Traits: Curious Matteo
slides.text:
Character traits are the qualities of a person.
We infer traits by looking at character choices.
Matteo looks closely at the pond to find things.
He asks questions about who is hiding under leaves.
We can infer that Matteo is a curious boy.
He acts like a scientist who loves to explore.
Understanding traits makes story characters feel real.
What traits do you show when you do experiments?

examples.title: Infer
examples.content: Asks questions -> Matteo is curious.

slides.title: Grammar: Possessive Pronouns
slides.text:
Possessive pronouns show who owns an object.
They tell us who the object belongs to in a sentence.
The pronouns are: my, your, his, her, and its.
'This is my magnifying glass.' (belongs to me).
'That is his science notebook.' (belongs to him).
'She plays with her green balloon.' (belongs to her).
'The dog wagged its tail.' (belongs to the dog).
Let's practice showing ownership in our sentences!

examples.title: Ownership
examples.content: my book, his net, her drum

slides.title: Interrogative vs Possessive
slides.text:
Interrogative and possessive pronouns work together.
We use an interrogative pronoun to ask a question:
'Whose science book is on the desk?'
We use a possessive pronoun to give the answer:
'It is her science book on the desk.'
This shows how questions and answers connect.
Always use the correct pronoun for the character.
Whose pencil are you using to write today?

examples.title: Interrogative
examples.content: Whose

examples.title: Possessive
examples.content: her

slides.title: Science Connection: Animal Habitats
slides.text:
Where does the crab crawl during the day?
It lives in its sandy shoreline habitat.
The question 'Where does the crab live?' is interrogative.
It ends with a question mark and asks about place.
The answer is a declarative sentence telling a fact:
'The crab crawls in the wet sand.'
We use verbs like 'crawls' to show its action.
We use nouns like 'sand' to describe its habitat.

examples.title: Observation
examples.content: A crab crawling in the wet sand.

slides.title: Science Connection: Water Safety
slides.text:
Why must we keep the drain plug clean?
A clean plug lets the water flow safely away.
The question 'Why do we clean it?' is interrogative.
The word 'flow' is an action verb for water.
In science, we ask questions about water movement.
We observe how liquid flows down the pipe.
The word 'plug' is our CCVC spelling focus.
We must spell it correctly as p - l - u - g!

examples.title: Action Verb
examples.content: flow, drain, clean

slides.title: Guided Practice: Spotting CCVC
slides.text:
Let's play a game to spot CCVC words.
Read this: 'The child sat on a flat rock.'
Which word starts with a double consonant blend?
Yes, the word is 'flat'! It starts with 'fl'.
The other words do not follow the CCVC pattern.
Spelling 'flat' correctly requires the letters f-l-a-t.
Can you find another CCVC word in your house?
Look at the flag or the drain plug!

examples.title: Check
examples.content: f - l - a - t starts with the consonants f and l.

slides.title: Guided Practice: Finding the Verb
slides.text:
Let's practice finding action words in sentences.
Read: 'The blue bird flies high in the sky.'
What is the bird doing in the sentence?
It is flying! So the verb is 'flies'.
The other words are nouns, adjectives, or pronouns.
The verb 'flies' tells us the action of the subject.
Always look for the action word to find the verb.
What verb describes what a fish does in water?

examples.title: Verb Check
examples.content: flies (action performed by the bird)

slides.title: Guided Practice: Question Mark
slides.text:
Let's check the rules for asking sentences.
Which sentence is punctuated correctly today?
'A. Where is the green frog.' or
'B. Where is the green frog?'
B is correct because it asks a question!
It must end with a question mark, not a period.
Always check the ending mark of your questions.
It tells the reader how to say the sentence!

examples.title: Corrected
examples.content: 'Where is the green frog?'

slides.title: Common Error: Capitalizing Pronouns
slides.text:
A common mistake is capitalizing possessive pronouns.
Do not write: 'This is My green frog.'
Instead, write: 'This is my green frog.'
Pronouns like 'my' or 'her' do not need capitals.
They only start with a capital if they begin a sentence.
E.g., 'My frog is green.' is correct.
Keep pronouns lowercase when they are in the middle!
Let's practice writing them correctly in our logs.

examples.title: Incorrect
examples.content: 'This is My frog.'

examples.title: Correct
examples.content: 'This is my frog.'

slides.title: Summary of Week 2
slides.text:
Congratulations! You completed all the slides for Week 2.
Let's review the main language tools we mastered.
We learned to decode CCVC words like frog, crab, and plug.
We learned to identify action verbs in our sentences.
We learned to write interrogative sentences with question marks.
We learned to infer character feelings and traits from clues.
We connected our English lessons to animal observations. 
You are now ready to take the quiz and challenges!

examples.title: Vocabulary
examples.content: frog, drum, plug, stop, crab, flag, slip, swim, flat, trap

standard.q: Which word starts with a CCVC blend?
Identify the CCVC word in this sentence: 'The crab crawls.'
Complete the word: 'The band played a loud dr__.'

Which CCVC word represents a device that blocks water in a sink?
Which of these is an action word (verb)?

Which verb shows what fish do to move in water?
Complete: 'Matteo ___ his science notebook every night.'
Which of these is an interrogative sentence?
options: The frog lives in the pond.

standard.q: What punctuation mark is used at the end of an interrogative sentence?
Complete the question: '___ is the color of the crab?'
Complete the question: '___ does the water flow when it rains?'
Which of these is an asking sentence that is punctuated correctly?
options: Who is hiding under the leaf?
who is hiding under the leaf.
Who is hiding under the leaf.
who is hiding under the leaf?

standard.q: Read: 'Matteo jumps and cheers when he sees the frog.' How does Matteo feel?
Read: 'The bird flew away when the cat came close.' What trait does the bird show?
If a character shares their lunch with a hungry classmate, we can infer they are:
What does it mean to 'infer' a trait?
options: To use clues to guess character qualities
To write a word down on paper
To count the number of characters
To make a drawing of the setting

standard.q: Read: 'Matteo stays up to check on his plants.' We can infer that Matteo is:
Complete: 'Whose science book is this?' 'It is ___ book (belongs to me).'
Complete: 'The dog wagged ___ tail when it saw the water.'
Complete: '___ is looking at the pond?' 'Matteo is looking.'
Complete: 'She plays with ___ doll in the garden (belongs to her).'
Identify the interrogative pronoun in: 'What did you find in the soil?'

challenge.q: Challenge: Which sentence contains a CCVC word, an action verb, and a possessive pronoun?
options: The frog swims in my pond.
The dog barked at the cat.
Can you see the blue flag?

challenge.q: Challenge: If you want to ask a friend about the habitat of a crab, which question is correct?
options: The crab lives on the beach.

challenge.q: Challenge: Read: 'The frog sat still. It did not move even when a fly buzzed by. Soon, its head dropped.' What can we infer about the frog?
options: It is very tired or resting.
It is angry at the fly.

challenge.q: Challenge: Which CCVC word represents a flat fabric used to show a country or community symbol?
Challenge: Spot the error: 'whose green frog is that?'
options: The first letter should be capitalized.
It should end with a period.

performance.title: Animal Movement Observation Net
performance.desc: Go outside to observe a small animal or insect (like an ant, a bird, or a frog). Draw the animal in its habitat. Write three interrogative sentences asking questions about how it moves, what it eats, and who shares its habitat. Identify and underline the action verbs in your questions.
labels: Correct identification of animal behavior and drawing
Correct form and punctuation of interrogative sentences (starts with capital, ends with ?)
Underlining the correct action verbs (e.g. crawl, fly, eat, hop)

pages:
Exercise 1: CCVC Phonics Hunt

Write the correct CCVC word that matches each animal or object clue below using your stylus.

1. An amphibian that leaps and lives in wet ponds: fr__
frog
2. A hard-shelled animal that crawls sideways on wet sand: cr__
crab
3. Moving in water using fins or limbs: sw__
swim

Exercise 2: Action Verbs in Nature

Identify the action verb (movement word) in each sentence and write it down.

1. The brown dog swims in the deep river.
swims
2. A heavy rock rolls down the muddy hill.
rolls
3. Matteo reads a science book about habitats.
reads

Exercise 3: Asking Questions (Interrogatives)

Rearrange the words to build a correct interrogative sentence. Don't forget capital letters and question marks!

1. frog / where / the / does / live
Where does the frog live?

filipino.title: Filipino
filipino.subtitle: Week 2: Pangngalan sa Tao, Bagay, Hayop, at Pangyayari

slides.title: Maligayang Pagdating sa Week 2!
slides.text: Kumusta ka, mag-aaral? Welcome sa ikalawang linggo! Ngayong linggo, palalawakin natin ang ating kaalaman. Pag-aaralan natin ang iba pang kategorya ng pangngalan. Ito ang ngalan ng tao, bagay, hayop, at pangyayari. Tukuyin natin ang pambalana at pantangi sa bawat isa. Sanayin din natin ang ating sarili sa tamang pagsulat. Napaka-kapaki-pakinabang nito sa pagbuo ng pangungusap. Simulan natin ang talakayan nang may sigla ngayon!

examples.title: Layunin
examples.content: Matukoy ang pambalana at pantangi sa tao, bagay, hayop, at pangyayari.

slides.title: Ngalan ng Tao sa Balarila
slides.text: Ang tao ay may iba't ibang papel sa ating buhay. Ang pangkalahatang tawag sa kanila ay pambalana. Halimbawa: guro, doktor, bayani, pulis, at nanay. Ang mga salitang ito ay nagsisimula sa maliit na titik. Ang tiyak na pangalan ng tao naman ay pantangi. Halimbawa: Dr. Jose Rizal, Gng. Santos, Matteo. Ito ay isinusulat na laging may malaking titik. Sino ang paborito mong guro sa paaralan ngayon?

examples.title: Tao
examples.content: guro (teacher - pambalana), Dr. Jose Rizal (proper)

slides.title: Ngalan ng Bagay sa Balarila
slides.text: Ang bagay ay mga walang-buhay na gamit natin. Ang pangkalahatang tawag sa bagay ay pambalana. Halimbawa: sapatos, gatas, kotse, papel, at lapis. Isinusulat natin ang mga ito sa maliit na titik. Ngunit ang tiyak na tatak o brand nito ay pantangi. Halimbawa: Nike, Bear Brand, Toyota, Mongol. Lagi nating lakihan ang unang titik ng tatak. Tingnan ang iyong lapis ngayon at suriin ang tatak!

examples.title: Bagay
examples.content: gatas (milk - pambalana), Bear Brand (proper)

slides.title: Ngalan ng Hayop sa Balarila
slides.text: Ang mga hayop ay may buhay ring kasama natin. Ang pangkalahatang tawag sa hayop ay pambalana. Halimbawa: aso, pusa, ibon, kalabaw, at isda. Ang mga ito ay isinusulat sa maliit na titik. Kung bibigyan natin sila ng pangalan, ito ay pantangi. Halimbawa: Bantay, Muning, Tagpi, Tweetie. Isinusulat ang pangalan ng alagang hayop sa malaki. Mayroon ka bang alagang hayop sa inyong bahay?

examples.title: Hayop
examples.content: aso (dog - pambalana), Bantay (proper)

slides.title: Ngalan ng Pangyayari
slides.text: Ang pangyayari ay mga okasyon na ating dinaranas. Ang pangkalahatang tawag sa okasyon ay pambalana. Halimbawa: kaarawan, kasal, binyag, at pista. Ngunit ang tiyak na pagdiriwang ay pantangi. Halimbawa: Pasko, Bagong Taon, Araw ng Kalayaan. Dahil sila ay mga tiyak at tanging okasyon sa taon. Kailangan natin silang isulat gamit ang malaking titik. Ano ang pinakagusto mong pagdiriwang sa inyong bayan?

examples.title: Pangyayari
examples.content: pista (festival - pambalana), Pasko (proper)

slides.title: Tao: Pangkalahatan at Tiyak
slides.text: Mag-ehersisyo tayo sa pagkilala ng ngalan ng tao. Basahin: 'Si Gng. Santos ay isang mabait na guro.' Ang 'guro' dito ay pambalana dahil ito ay tungkulin. Ang 'Gng. Santos' ay pantangi dahil ito ay tiyak na ngalan. Dapat na malaki ang G at malaki rin ang S sa Santos. Ito ang wastong paraan upang isulat ang guro natin. Nakatutulong ito sa pagpapakita ng galang sa kanila. Sumulat ng pangungusap tungkol sa iyong nanay!

examples.title: Tao Pagsasanay
examples.content: guro (pambalana) -> Gng. Santos (pantangi)

slides.title: Bagay: Tiyak na Tatak
slides.text: Tingnan natin ang mga sapatos na suot mo ngayon. Ang salitang 'sapatos' ay isang pangngalang pambalana. Kung ang sapatos ay tatak Nike, paano ito isusulat? Isusulat natin ito na 'Nike' gamit ang malaking N. Hindi natin isusulat ang brand sa maliit na titik. Ang mga tatak ng gatas tulad ng 'Bear Brand' ay gayundin. Malaki ang titik B sa Bear at malaki sa Brand. Suriin ang mga tatak ng pagkain sa inyong kusina!

examples.title: Bagay Pagsasanay
examples.content: sapatos (pambalana) -> Nike (pantangi)

slides.title: Hayop: Pangalan ng Alaga
slides.text: Mayroon ka bang paboritong aso o pusa sa bahay? Ang salitang 'aso' at 'pusa' ay mga pambalana lamang. Ngunit kung tinawag mo ang iyong aso na 'Bantay', ang 'Bantay' ay magiging pangngalang pantangi sa wika. Dapat itong isulat na malaki ang unang titik na B. Halimbawa: 'Masaya si Bantay habang kumakain ng pagkain.' Ang 'Bantay' ay may malaking B; ang 'pagkain' ay pambalana. Tandaan ang panuntunan sa inyong sariling alaga!

examples.title: Hayop Pagsasanay
examples.content: pusa (pambalana) -> Muning (pantangi)

slides.title: Pangyayari: Mga Pagdiriwang
slides.text: Ang bawat taon ay puno ng masasayang okasyon. Ang 'kaarawan' ay pambalana sa ating wika. Milyun-milyong tao ang nagdiriwang ng kaarawan. Ngunit ang 'Pasko' ay tiyak na okasyon sa Disyembre. Kaya ang 'Pasko' ay pangngalang pantangi sa wika. Isinusulat natin ang Pasko na may malaking titik P. Gayundin ang 'Bagong Taon' na may malaking B at T. Wasto bang isulat ang okasyon sa maliit na titik?

examples.title: Pangyayari Pagsasanay
examples.content: kasal (wedding - pambalana), Pasko (proper)

slides.title: Guided Practice: Tao
slides.text: Suriin natin ang mga tao sa ating paligid ngayon. Sino ang nagtuturo sa atin sa silid-aralan? Siya ay ang ating 'guro' na isang pambalana. Kung ang pangalan niya ay Gng. Cruz, paano isusulat? Isusulat natin ito na 'Gng. Cruz' sa malalaking titik. Ang 'Gng.' ay may malaking G at ang 'Cruz' ay malaking C. Ito ay nagpapakita ng tamang pagsulat ng pantangi. Sanayin ito sa pagsusulat ng pangalan ng iyong ama!

examples.title: Guro
examples.content: guro (pambalana) -> Gng. Cruz (pantangi)

slides.title: Guided Practice: Bagay
slides.text: Hawakan mo ang iyong lapis na ginagamit sa pagsulat. Ang salitang 'lapis' ay pangkalahatang bagay o pambalana. Ano ang tatak ng iyong lapis na nakasulat dito? Maaaring ito ay 'Mongol' na isang pangngalang pantangi. Kailangan nating simulan ang Mongol sa malaking titik M. Ito ay panuntunan sa pagsulat ng tatak ng bagay. Isulat sa iyong notebook ang tatak ng iyong lapis! Ipakita sa iyong katabi ang iyong naisulat ngayon!

examples.title: Lapis
examples.content: lapis (pambalana) -> Mongol (pantangi)

slides.title: Guided Practice: Hayop
slides.text: Suriin natin ang aso na tumatahol sa kalsada. Ang 'aso' ay pangkalahatang tawag sa hayop na ito. Kaya ang aso ay isang pangngalang pambalana. Kung ang aso ni Matteo ay pinangalanang 'Tagpi', ang salitang 'Tagpi' ay pantangi na may malaking T. Isusulat natin si Tagpi nang may wastong malaking titik. Ito ay dahil si Tagpi ay may sariling pangalan. Maaari mo bang isulat ang pangalan ng iyong aso?

examples.title: Aso
examples.content: aso (pambalana) -> Tagpi (pantangi)

slides.title: Guided Practice: Pangyayari
slides.text: Isipin natin ang okasyon ng Pasko sa Disyembre. Ang salitang 'Pasko' ay tiyak na pangyayari o okasyon. Kaya ito ay isang pangngalang pantangi sa Filipino. Paano natin isusulat ang salitang Pasko sa papel? Isusulat natin ito na 'Pasko' na may malaking titik P. Ang salitang 'pagdiriwang' naman ay pambalana lamang. Isinusulat ang pagdiriwang sa maliit na titik p. Tandaan ang dalawang anyo na ito sa pagsusulat!

examples.title: Pasko
examples.content: pagdiriwang (pambalana) -> Pasko (pantangi)

slides.title: Pagwawasto sa Pangungusap
slides.text: Suriin natin ang mga titik sa pangungusap na ito. Mali: 'Bumili si matteo ng sapatos na nike.' Bakit mali ang pagsulat ng pangungusap na ito? Dahil ang 'matteo' at 'nike' ay mga pantangi. Dapat silang magsimula sa malaking titik M at N. Tama: 'Bumili si Matteo ng sapatos na Nike.' Ang 'sapatos' ay nananatiling maliit ang titik s. Ito ang wastong balarila na dapat nating sundin!

examples.title: Pagwawasto
examples.content: matteo -> Matteo, nike -> Nike

slides.title: Maling Pagsulat ng Hayop
slides.text: Iwasan ang pagsusulat ng pangalan ng hayop sa maliit. Mali: 'Naglalaro ang pusa kong si muning sa bahay.' Dahil ang 'muning' ay tiyak na ngalan ng iyong pusa. Dapat itong isulat na 'Muning' na may malaking M. Tama: 'Naglalaro ang pusa kong si Muning sa bahay.' Ang salitang 'pusa' ay nananatiling maliit ang titik p. Suriin ang iyong pagsulat upang maiwasan ang mali! Ang tamang pagsulat ay nagpapakita ng husay sa wika!

examples.title: Hayop Pagsusuri
examples.content: muning -> Muning, pusa -> pusa

slides.title: Maling Pagsulat ng Bagay
slides.text: Huwag lakihan ang unang titik ng pangkalahatang bagay. Mali: 'Kumuha ako ng Lapis sa aking bag kanina.' Ang 'lapis' ay pangkalahatang pambalana lamang sa wika. Kaya dapat itong isulat sa maliit na titik l. Tama: 'Kumuha ako ng lapis sa aking bag kanina.' Gayundin ang 'bag', maliit na titik b ang dapat. Tiyakin na pambalana ay maliit na titik lagi! Ito ang susi sa malinis at wastong pagsusulat!

examples.title: Bagay Pagsusuri
examples.content: Lapis -> lapis, Bag -> bag

slides.title: Kategorya ng Tao: Mga Tungkulin
slides.text: Ang mga tungkulin ng tao ay mga pambalana. Halimbawa: inhinyero, nars, bumbero, at sundalo. Isinusulat natin ang mga ito sa maliit na titik. Ngunit ang tiyak na ngalan ng sundalo ay pantangi. Halimbawa: 'Sgt. Ramos' ay isang pangngalang pantangi. Nagsisimula ang Ramos sa malaking titik na R. Ito ay nagpapakita ng tiyak na pagkakakilanlan niya. Kilalanin ang mga tungkulin ng tao sa komunidad!

examples.title: Tungkulin
examples.content: sundalo (pambalana) -> Sgt. Ramos (pantangi)

slides.title: Kategorya ng Bagay: Mga Laruan
slides.text: Ang mga laruan ay kabilang sa ngalan ng bagay. Ang 'laruan' at 'manika' ay mga pambalana. Maraming laruan ang makikita sa silid ng bata. Ngunit ang tiyak na tatak ng laruan ay pantangi. Halimbawa: 'Barbie' o 'Lego' ay mga pantangi. Kailangan natin silang simulan sa malalaking titik. Ito ay nagpapakita ng tatak ng laruan sa wika. Ano ang tatak ng iyong paboritong laruan ngayon?

examples.title: Laruan
examples.content: manika (doll - pambalana), Barbie (proper)

slides.title: Kategorya ng Hayop: Mga Ibon
slides.text: Ang mga ibon ay mga hayop na lumilipad sa hangin. Ang salitang 'ibon' at 'loro' ay mga pambalana. Ang agila ay pambalana rin sa ating balarila. Ngunit kung may tiyak na ngalan ang alagang loro, tulad ng 'Polly', ito ay magiging pantangi. Isusulat natin si Polly na may malaking titik P. Ito ay nagpapakita ng pangalan ng iyong alagang ibon. Suriin ang mga ibon sa inyong kapaligiran ngayon!

examples.title: Ibon
examples.content: ibon (bird - pambalana), Polly (proper)

slides.title: Kategorya ng Pangyayari: Mga Pista
slides.text: Ang pista ay isang mahalagang pangyayari sa bayan. Ang salitang 'pista' ay isang pangngalang pambalana. Maraming bayan ang may kani-kanilang pista taon-taon. Ngunit ang 'Pista ng Pahiyas' ay pantangi sa wika. Ito ay tiyak na okasyon na ipinagdiriwang sa Lucban. Isinusulat natin ang Pahiyas sa malaking titik P. Ito ay nagpapakita ng tiyak na ngalan ng okasyon. Nakadalo ka na ba sa pista sa inyong lalawigan?

examples.title: Pista
examples.content: pista (pambalana) -> Pista ng Pahiyas (pantangi)

slides.title: Pagsasanay sa Kategorya: Tao
slides.text: Tukuyin natin ang kategorya ng tao sa pagsasanay. Basahin: 'Si Dr. Rizal ay isang mahusay na doktor.' Ano ang pangkalahatang ngalan ng tao sa pahayag? Ang pangkalahatang ngalan ay 'doktor' na pambalana. Ano ang tiyak na ngalan ng tao sa pahayag? Ang tiyak na ngalan ay 'Dr. Rizal' na pantangi. Nagsisimula ang Dr. Rizal sa malaking titik D at R. Ito ay nagpapakita ng tamang kategorya ng tao ngayon!

examples.title: Pagsasanay Tao
examples.content: doktor (pambalana) -> Dr. Rizal (pantangi)

slides.title: Pagsasanay sa Kategorya: Bagay
slides.text: Suriin natin ang mga bagay na ginagamit sa klase. Basahin: 'Ginamit niya ang krayola na Crayola.' Ang unang 'krayola' ay pangkalahatang pambalana. Ang ikalawang 'Crayola' ay tiyak na tatak o pantangi. Kailangan ang malaking titik C sa tatak na Crayola. Ang pambalana ay isinusulat sa maliit na titik k. Ito ay nagpapakita ng pagkakaiba sa pagsulat ng dalawa. Maaari mo ba itong kopyahin sa iyong papel ngayon?

examples.title: Pagsasanay Bagay
examples.content: krayola (pambalana) -> Crayola (pantangi)

slides.title: Pagsasanay sa Kategorya: Hayop
slides.text: Tukuyin natin ang mga hayop sa pangungusap na ito. Basahin: 'May alagang pusa si Matteo na si Muning.' Ang 'pusa' ay pangkalahatang tawag sa hayop na ito. Kaya ito ay isang pangngalang pambalana sa wika. Ang 'Muning' ay tiyak na pangalan ng alagang hayop. Kaya ito ay pangngalang pantangi na may malaking M. Isulat ang pangungusap na ito nang may wastong titik! Ipakita ang iyong galing sa pagtukoy ng hayop ngayon!

examples.title: Pagsasanay Hayop
examples.content: pusa (pambalana) -> Muning (pantangi)

slides.title: Pagsasanay sa Kategorya: Pangyayari
slides.text: Suriin natin ang mga okasyon na ipinagdiriwang natin. Basahin: 'Masaya ang bawat pista tulad ng Pahiyas.' Ang 'pista' ay pangkalahatang ngalan ng pangyayari. Kaya ito ay isang pangngalang pambalana sa balarila. Ang 'Pahiyas' ay tiyak na ngalan ng pagdiriwang natin. Kaya ito ay isinusulat na may malaking titik P. Ito ay nagpapakita ng tiyak na ngalan ng pangyayari. Magsanay sa pagsulat ng mga okasyon sa iyong notebook!

examples.title: Pagsasanay Pangyayari
examples.content: pista (pambalana) -> Pahiyas (pantangi)

slides.title: Buod ng Ikalawang Linggo
slides.text: Binabati kita! Natapos mo ang lahat ng slide ngayon. Natutunan natin ang pambalana at pantangi sa tao. Natutunan din natin ang pambalana at pantangi sa bagay. Natutunan natin ang mga ito sa hayop at pangyayari. Nalaman natin ang wastong paggamit ng malalaking titik. Ginamit natin ang mga halimbawa ng tatak at alagang hayop. Handa ka na bang sumagot sa maikling pagsusulit natin? Ipakita ang iyong husay sa wikang Filipino ngayon!

examples.title: Tandaan
examples.content: Pantangi = Tiyak. Pambalana = Karaniwan. Gamitin ang malaking titik!

standard.q: Alin sa mga sumusunod ang pangngalang pantangi sa tao?
Alin sa mga sumusunod ang pangngalang pambalana sa bagay?
Ano ang tiyak na ngalan (pantangi) ng hayop?
Alin sa mga sumusunod ang pangkalahatang ngalan (pambalana) ng pangyayari?
Tukuyin ang pantangi sa tao sa pangungusap: 'Si Matteo ay nag-aaral ng leksyon.'
Ano ang pambalana ng 'Mongol'?
Ano ang pantangi ng 'pusa'?
Paano isinusulat ang tatak ng isang bagay tulad ng 'Toyota'?
options: Malaking titik sa simula
Lahat ay malalaking titik
Lahat ay maliliit na titik

standard.q: Tukuyin ang pambalana sa tao: 'Ang bumbero ay tumulong sa pamilya.'
Tama o Mali: Ang salitang 'sapatos' ay isang pangngalang pantangi.
Aling okasyon ang isang pangngalang pantangi sa pangyayari?
Tukuyin ang pantangi sa bagay sa pangungusap: 'Bumili si nanay ng bagong gatas na Bear Brand.'
Ano ang pambalana ng 'Dr. Jose Rizal'?
Alin sa mga sumusunod ang pambalana sa hayop na nagbibigay ng gatas?
Ano ang pantangi ng 'bagay'?
Tukuyin ang pambalana sa bagay: 'Naglalaro ang bata gamit ang kanyang pulang bola.'
Tama o Mali: Ang 'Pasko' ay isinusulat gamit ang maliit na titik p.
Alin sa mga sumusunod ang pambalana sa tao na nag-aalaga ng ngipin?
Aling salita ang may pagkakamali sa pagkakagamit ng malaking titik sa pangungusap: 'May alagang Pusa si Kiko.'?
Ano ang tinutukoy ng pangngalang hayop?
options: ngalan ng okasyon

standard.q: Alin ang pantangi ng 'kaarawan'?
Tukuyin ang pambalana sa hayop: 'Lumipad ang ibon sa sanga ng puno.'
Tama o Mali: Ang 'Mongol' ay isang pangngalang pantangi dahil ito ay tiyak na tatak ng lapis.
Alin sa mga sumusunod ang pambalana sa tao na nagtatanggol sa bayan?
Saan dapat magsimula ang pagsulat ng pangalan ng iyong alagang aso?

challenge.q: Challenge: Aling pangungusap ang naglalaman ng parehong pantangi sa tao at pantangi sa bagay?
options: Bumili si Matteo ng bagong sapatos na Nike.
Naglalaro ang aso sa malawak na kalsada.
Si Gng. Santos ay nagtuturo sa silid-aralan.
Masaya si Muning habang kumakain ng isda.

challenge.q: Challenge: Alin sa mga sumusunod ang may wastong pagsulat ng lahat ng pantangi?
Challenge: Kung ang 'laruan' ay pambalana, alin ang maaaring maging katapat nitong pantangi?
Challenge: Tukuyin kung gaano karaming pangngalan ang nasa kategorya ng bagay at tao sa pangungusap: 'Binigyan ng guro ang bata ng bagong lapis.'
Challenge: Suriin ang pahayag: 'Ipinagdiwang ang Pasko sa buong komunidad.' Ano ang kategorya ng 'Pasko' sa pangngalan?
options: Pantangi sa tao
Pambalana sa pangyayari
Pantangi sa pangyayari

performance.title: Paggawa ng Tsart ng Aking Pangngalan
performance.desc: Kumuha ng isang malinis na papel at hatiin ito sa apat na kolum: Tao, Bagay, Hayop, at Pangyayari. Sumulat ng dalawang pambalana at dalawang pantangi sa ilalim ng bawat kolum batay sa mga makikita sa inyong tahanan (hal. aso -> Bantay). Kulayan at palamutian ang iyong tsart, at ipaliwanag ito sa iyong magulang o kapatid.
labels: Maayos na nagawa ang tsart na may apat na kategorya
Wastong naisulat ang dalawang pambalana at pantangi sa bawat kolum
Wastong nagamit ang malaking titik sa lahat ng pangngalang pantangi

makabansa.title: Makabansa
makabansa.subtitle: Week 2: Mahahalagang Pook sa Ating Komunidad

slides.title: Maligayang Pagdating sa Week 2!
slides.text: Kumusta ka, mag-aaral? Welcome sa ikalawang linggo! Ngayong linggo, maglalakbay tayo sa ating sariling pook. Tukuyin natin ang mahahalagang pook sa ating komunidad. Kasama rito ang barangay hall, simbahan, at paaralan. Pag-aaralan natin ang tungkulin ng bawat pook na ito. Alamin din natin ang kahalagahan ng mga makasaysayang lugar. Magmasid at suriin ang iyong paligid na parang researcher! Handa ka na bang matuto tungkol sa ating komunidad?

examples.title: Layunin ng Linggo
examples.content: Matukoy ang mahahalagang pook sa sariling komunidad at ang kanilang tungkulin.

slides.title: Ang Barangay Hall: Sentro ng Pamamahala
slides.text: Ang barangay hall ay isang napakahalagang pook sa atin. Ito ang sentro ng pamamahala sa ating sariling komunidad. Dito nagpupulong ang kapitan at mga barangay kagawad. Gumagawa sila ng mga batas upang mapanatili ang kapayapaan. Dito rin pumupunta ang mamamayan kapag may problema. Nagbibigay sila ng mga tulong at serbisyo sa lahat. Ang barangay hall ay simbolo ng pagkakaisa at serbisyo. Saan matatagpuan ang barangay hall sa inyong pook?

examples.title: Barangay Hall
examples.content: Dito naglilingkod ang Barangay Kapitan at mga Kagawad.

slides.title: Ang Health Center: Pook ng Kalusugan
slides.text: Ang health center ay pook na nag-aalaga sa kalusugan. Dito pumupunta ang mga sanggol, bata, at matatanda. Mayroong mga nars, doktor, at midwives na naglilingkod. Nagbibigay sila ng libreng bakuna at mga gamot sa amin. Sinusuri nila ang timbang at kalagayan ng mga sanggol. Nakatutulong ito upang maiwasan ang sakit sa komunidad. Ang health center ay bukas para sa lahat ng mamamayan. Nakarating ka na ba sa health center sa inyong pook?

examples.title: Health Center
examples.content: Pook para sa libreng bakuna, gamot, at pagsusuri ng kalusugan.

slides.title: Ang Paaralan: Pook ng Karunungan
slides.text: Ang paaralan ay pook kung saan tayo nakakukuha ng dunong. Dito nagtuturo ang mga guro sa mga mag-aaral. Natututo tayong magbasa, magsulat, at magkuwenta rito. Kasama rito ang pag-aaral ng science at kasaysayan. Mayroon tayong silid-aralan, aklatan, at palaruan. Ang paaralan ay naghahanda sa atin para sa hinaharap. Ito ay simbolo ng pag-asa at pag-unlad ng pamayanan. Ano ang pangalan ng inyong sariling paaralan ngayon?

examples.title: Paaralan
examples.content: Tahanan ng karunungan at paggabay ng mga guro.

slides.title: Ang Simbahan: Pook ng Pananampalataya
slides.text: Ang simbahan o moske ay pook para sa pananalangin. Dito nagtitipon ang pamilya upang magpasalamat sa Diyos. Itinuturo rito ang mabuting asal at pagmamahal sa kapwa. May mga pari, pastor, o imam na namumuno sa pagsamba. Ang bawat pook-pagsamba ay dapat nating igalang palagi. Sinasalamin nito ang pananampalataya ng mga mamamayan. Mayroon ding mga kapilya sa mas maliliit na sitio. Saan kayo madalas magsimba o sumamba ng inyong pamilya?

examples.title: Pook-Pagsamba
examples.content: simbahan, moske, kapilya, o templo sa komunidad.

slides.title: Ang Palengke: Pook ng Kalakalan
slides.text: Ang palengke ay sentro ng kalakalan sa komunidad natin. Dito pumupunta ang mga tindero upang magbenta ng ani. Kasama rito ang isda, karne, gulay, at mga prutas. Dito rin pumupunta ang mga mamamayan upang bumili ng ulam. Nakatutulong ang palengke sa kabuhayan ng maraming tao. Mayroon ding mga maliliit na tindahan sa bawat kalye. Ang palengke ay nagpapakita ng kasiglahan ng ekonomiya. Sino ang kasama mong pumunta sa palengke noong Lunes?

examples.title: Palengke
examples.content: Pook kung saan nagaganap ang pagbili at pagbebenta.

slides.title: Ang Plaza at Parke: Pook ng Libangan
slides.text: Ang plaza ay isang malawak at bukas na pook sa bayan. Dito naglalaro ang mga bata tuwing hapon pagkatapos ng klase. Dito rin ginaganap ang mga programa at pista ng barangay. May mga puno, upuan, at kung minsan ay wind vane. Ito ay pook para magpahinga at makipag-usap sa kapwa. Nakatutulong ang parke sa kalusugan ng ating katawan. Dapat nating panatilihing malinis ang ating plaza palagi. Ano ang paborito mong laro sa plaza kasama ang kaibigan?

examples.title: Plaza
examples.content: Pook-pasyalan para sa pamilya at mga pagdiriwang.

slides.title: Mga Makasaysayang Monumento
slides.text: May mga pook na nagpapaalala sa ating mga bayani. Tinatawag natin silang mga makasaysayang monumento sa bayan. Halimbawa: ang monumento ni Dr. Rizal sa plaza ng bayan. Ito ay yari sa matigas na bato at makintab na metal. Ipinapakita nito ang kadakilaan ng mga nagtanggol sa atin. Saksi ang mga pook na ito sa mahahalagang digmaan. Dapat nating igalang ang mga monumento at huwag sulatan. Mayroon bang monumento sa plaza ng inyong lungsod?

examples.title: Monumento
examples.content: Bantayog ng bayani upang alalahanin ang nakaraan.

slides.title: Ang Police Outpost: Pook ng Seguridad
slides.text: Ang police outpost o barangay tanod outpost ay pook. Dito nagbabantay ang mga pulis at tanod sa gabi. Sila ang nagpapanatili ng seguridad sa buong pook natin. Tinitiyak nila na walang krimen o gulo sa mga kalye. Maaari tayong tumakbo sa kanila kapag natatakot tayo. Sila ay mga tagapagtanggol ng kapayapaan sa komunidad. Ang outpost ay may emergency numbers na nakasulat sa dingding. Suriin ang lokasyon ng pinakamalapit na outpost ngayon!

examples.title: Seguridad
examples.content: Pook para sa kaligtasan at paghingi ng tulong sa pulis.

slides.title: Ang Bahay-Pamahalaan ng Bayan
slides.text: Ang munisipyo o kapitolyo ay mas malaking pook. Ito ang bahay-pamahalaan ng buong bayan o lungsod. Dito nagtatrabaho ang alkalde o mayor ng bayan natin. Dito ginagawa ang mga lisensya at malalaking batas. Ang munisipyo ay mas malaki kaysa sa barangay hall. Karaniwan itong matatagpuan sa sentro ng bayan natin. May magandang logo ng bayan sa harap ng gusali nito. Nakarating ka na ba sa munisipyo ng inyong bayan?

examples.title: Munisipyo
examples.content: Sentral na tanggapan ng Mayor at pamahalaan ng bayan.

slides.title: Selyo at Logo ng Ating Pook
slides.text: Ang bawat mahahalagang pook ay may selyo o logo. Makikita natin ito sa barangay hall at munisipyo natin. Ang selyo ay naglalaman ng mga simbolo ng pamumuhay. May simbolo ng palay, isda, o gulong ng makina. Ang palay ay sumasagisag sa saganang pagsasaka sa pook. Ang gear naman ay para sa mga makabagong pabrika natin. Ang selyo ay nagpapatunay ng opisyal na dokumento. Suriin ang mga simbolo sa selyo ng inyong bayan ngayon!

examples.title: Selyo ng Bayan
examples.content: Opisyal na logo na kumakatawan sa kasaysayan ng bayan.

slides.title: Pook ng Likas na Yaman
slides.text: May mga pook na pinagkukunan ng likas na yaman. Halimbawa: ang lawa ng Laguna de Bay sa lalawigan. Ito ay pook-pangingisda para sa maraming pamilya. Ang mga bukid sa tabi ng bundok ay para sa palay. Nagbibigay ito ng pagkain sa buong komunidad natin. Ang pangangalaga sa mga pook na ito ay tungkulin natin. Huwag magtapon ng basura sa ilog at lawa ng bayan. Paano mo mapoprotektahan ang mga likas na pook na ito?

examples.title: Likas na Yaman
examples.content: lawa (lake), bukid (fields), kagubatan (forests)

slides.title: Guided Practice: Pook at Tungkulin
slides.text: Magsanay tayo sa pag-uugnay ng pook at tungkulin. Suriin: 'Saan pumupunta ang may-sakit para sa gamot?' Ang sagot ay health center o ospital ng komunidad. Suriin: 'Saan nag-aaral ang mga bata ng science?' Ang sagot ay paaralan na pook ng karunungan natin. Suriin: 'Saan nagpupulong ang kapitan ng barangay?' Ang sagot ay barangay hall na sentro ng pamamahala. Nagawa mo bang iugnay ang lahat ng tungkulin ngayon?

examples.title: Pagsasanay
examples.content: ospital -> kalusugan, paaralan -> karunungan, hall -> pamamahala

slides.title: Guided Practice: Simbolo sa Logo
slides.text: Suriin natin ang logo ng munisipyo sa ating bayan. May simbolo ng araw na may walong sinag sa logo. Ano ang ibig sabihin ng araw sa kasaysayan natin? Ito ay sumasagisag sa pag-asa at kalayaan ng bansa. May simbolo rin ng bundok na may berdeng puno roon. Ipinapakita nito na mayaman sa kagubatan ang pook natin. Ang pag-unawa sa logo ay nagpapakita ng kaalaman natin. Bilugan ang simbolo ng araw sa inyong logo ngayon!

examples.title: Pagsasanay Simbolo
examples.content: araw (freedom), bundok (forest resources)

slides.title: Guided Practice: Makasaysayang Lugar
slides.text: Suriin natin ang isang makasaysayang pook sa Laguna. Ito ay ang 'Bahay ni Rizal' sa Lungsod ng Calamba. Dito lumaki si Jose Rizal kasama ang kanyang pamilya. Ang bahay na ito ay gawa sa matigas na bato at kahoy. Pinangangalagaan ito ng pamahalaan bilang monumento natin. Maraming turista ang pumupunta rito upang matuto. Ito ay isang primaryang batayan ng kasaysayan sa pook. Kopyahin ang pangalan ng pook na ito sa inyong papel!

examples.title: Pagsasanay Pook
examples.content: Bahay ni Rizal (Calamba) -> makasaysayang pook

slides.title: Maling Pagtukoy sa Tungkulin
slides.text: Iwasan ang pagpapalit ng tungkulin ng mga pook. Mali: 'Pumunta kami sa barangay hall upang magpagamot.' Ang barangay hall ay hindi klinika o ospital sa pook. Dapat pumunta sa health center kapag may karamdaman. Tama: 'Pumunta kami sa health center upang magpagamot.' Ang bawat pook ay may kani-kanilang tumpak na tungkulin. Tiyakin ang tamang pook bago sumulat sa iyong log ngayon! Ito ay nagpapakita ng tumpak na pag-unawa sa aralin!

examples.title: Tungkulin Error
examples.content: mali: hall -> gamot, tama: health center -> gamot

slides.title: Maling Paglalarawan ng Pook-Samba
slides.text: Iwasan ang kawalan ng galang sa mga pook-pagsamba. Huwag sabihing ang simbahan ay palaruan ng mga bata. Ito ay pook para sa pananalangin at tahimik na pagsamba. Dapat maging tahimik at maayos ang kilos sa loob nito. Gayundin sa loob ng mga moske ng ating kapatid na Muslim. Igalang natin ang lahat ng paniniwala sa komunidad natin. Ang paggalang sa pook ay nagpapakita ng mabuting asal. Tiyakin na wasto ang iyong paglalarawan sa papel ngayon!

examples.title: Pook-Samba
examples.content: Igalang ang simbahan, moske, at kapilya sa lahat ng oras.

slides.title: Karagdagang Pagsasanay 1
slides.text: Mag-review tayo ng mga aralin sa linggong ito. Pinag-aralan natin ang mahahalagang konsepto ng Week 2. Maging maingat sa pagbabasa at pag-unawa ng mga salita. Gamitin ang iyong kaalaman sa iyong sariling buhay. Maaari mong talakayin ito kasama ang iyong mga kalaro. Ang pag-aaral ng wika at asal ay nagpapalaki sa atin. Subukang sagutin ang lahat ng tanong sa pagsusulit natin. Ipagpatuloy ang pagiging mahusay at masipag na mag-aaral!

examples.title: Pagsasanay 1
examples.content: Ibahagi ang iyong mga natutunan sa aralin sa iyong pamilya ngayon.
